content: The 2024-2025 SOTA trend of structured, retrival-augmented active memory for active learning agents can be repurposed to solve the core limitation of intrinsic reward methods like ICM and RND: the unstructured 'curiosity drift' where agents fixate on noisy, uninformative stimuli. We hypothesize that adding an episodic memory gate that prioritizes exploration of memory gaps aligned to the agent’s downstream active learning task will improve sample efficiency of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning by 30-40% on long-horizon embodied tasks compared to vanilla ICM/RND.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
